shipped_spec_history.scope: AppThreat deep signals — EPSS scores (FIRST.org CSV) + CWE category rollup (MITRE Research Concepts catalog) shipped via Waves A + B; Phase T blint hardening + Phase U EPSS overlay phase + withdrawn-advisory filter CANCELLED at Wave C verify-don't-assume verification (low signal / redundant / vdb pre-filters at ingest); schema v28 → v24 → v25 round-trip cleanup

parts.description: Claude Code skill encoding the full conda-forge recipe lifecycle. 3-tier architecture: canonical scripts + CLI wrappers + data state. 10-step autonomous loop with one human-gated checkpoint at step 8b (prepare_submission_branch). Authoritative spine: SKILL.md.

subdirectories.SKILL.md: primary spine: critical constraints, 10-step autonomous loop, recipe authoring gotchas G1–G87
subdirectories.CHANGELOG.md: release history with TL;DR section (canonical drift-detection source)
subdirectories.reference/: 17 deep-reference files (recipe-yaml-reference[-full], meta-yaml-reference, mcp-tools, python-min-policy, conda-forge-yml-reference[-full], pinning-reference, selectors-reference, jinja-functions, abi3-matrix-collapse, dependency-input-formats, atlas-actionable-intelligence, atlas-phases-overview, atlas-phase-engineering, atlas-phase-p-cost-model, conda-forge-ecosystem)
subdirectories.guides/: 9 workflow guides (getting-started, migration, ci-troubleshooting, cross-compilation, feedstock-maintenance, feedstock-platform-expansion, testing-recipes, sdist-missing-license, atlas-operations)
subdirectories.scripts/: 54 canonical Python implementations (single source of truth for behavior); 46 thin CLI wrappers under .claude/scripts/conda-forge-expert/
subdirectories.templates/: 41 recipe scaffolding templates across 13 ecosystems (c-cpp, dotnet, fortran, go, java, multi-output, nodejs, perl, python, r, ruby, rust + conda-forge-yml/{staged-recipes,feedstock})
subdirectories.tests/: 82 test files (unit + integration + meta) — uses real fixtures, no network mocking

parts.description: Offline-tolerant package-intelligence pipeline that builds and maintains cf_atlas.db (SQLite, schema v28 as of 2026-06-20) inventorying conda-forge packages (32k working set) plus a separate pypi_universe side table (the full PyPI directory, ~800k rows) plus a pypi_intelligence side table (35-column enrichment layer indexed on pypi_name, 5 tiers) plus three external-catalog overlay tables: cisa_kev (CISA KEV catalogue, ~1,600 CVEs; v8.5.3+), epss_scores (FIRST.org EPSS daily CSV, ~334k CVEs; v8.6.0+), cwe_categories (MITRE CWE Research Concepts view with 67-entry cf_atlas seed mapping → 7 categories + 'Other'; v8.6.0+). 22 ordered phases (B→N + O/P/Q/R/S, with B.5/B.6/C.5/E.5/G' sub-phases) with TTL gates and checkpoint-aware resumption. Schema advanced v25 → v28 since the prior sync: v26 (pypi_downloads_daily, Phase P incremental BigQuery, v8.15.0), v27 (rolling 30/90d downloads + trend slope + per-platform/per-Python breakdown tables, Phase F Wave 2, v8.18.0), v28 (package_channel_downloads + platform/pyver/channel breakdown CLIs, Phase F Wave 3, v8.19.0). Air-gap-tolerant via S3-parquet (Phase F) and cf-graph offline (Phase H) backends. v7.9.0 actionable-scope audit closed phase-denominator drift across Phase H/J/M and split Phase D into daily-lean + TTL-gated universe upsert. v8.0.0 structural-enforcement + persona-profiles bundle ships schema v28's v_actionable_packages view (7 phase selectors refactored) + Phase H serial-aware eligible-rows gate (warm-daily ~5 min → ~30 s) + tests/meta/test_actionable_scope.py + bootstrap-data --profile {maintainer,admin,consumer} with gh-user + phase-L-sources auto-detection. v8.1.0 PyPI intelligence layer adds 5 new phases (O snapshot-based activity_band; P BigQuery downloads opt-in; Q cross-channel BOOLs; R per-project JSON enrichment for top-N candidate slice; S computed conda_forge_readiness + recommended_template), pypi-intelligence CLI + MCP tool, and the pypi_intelligence side table with v_pypi_candidates view. v8.5.3 (Path C) adds the cisa_kev overlay — DW13 close-out via direct CISA JSON pull (~2 MB / ~1,600 CVEs) instead of vdb --cache-os (which would have downloaded ~33 GB and still left vuln_kev_affecting_current=0 because appthreat-vulnerability-db hardcodes `kevc` in its DEFAULT_IGNORE_SOURCE_PATTERNS); Phase G + G' overlay loops OR vdb's per-CVE `kev` flag with the local KEV set. v8.5.3 (DW12) ships defense-in-depth rollup-staleness fix: new v_current_version_vulns query-time-correct view + _phase_g_sync_current_rollup pure-SQL tail step on Phase G'. v8.6.0 AppThreat Deep Signals release (3 waves shipped + Wave C cancelled): schema v28 → v24 → v25 round-trip cleanup adds epss_scores + cwe_categories side tables + 4 packages columns (vuln_max_epss_score, vuln_max_epss_percentile, vuln_cwe_top, vuln_cwe_categories_json) + 3 package_version_vulns columns; new fetch-epss + fetch-cwe-catalog CLIs (Path C pattern from v8.5.3 generalised); new _load_epss_scores + _load_cwe_categories helpers + shared _aggregate_v8_6_0_overlays pure function for Phase G/G' overlay loops; _phase_g_sync_current_rollup extended with COALESCE-to-existing review-fix on the new columns; 4 new flags across staleness-report / my-feedstocks / cve-watcher / detail-cf-atlas. Wave C (Phase T blint + Phase U EPSS overlay phase) cancelled pre-implementation at verify-don't-assume verification — Phase T low-signal in conda-forge's hermetic compile env (~0 hardening variance / ~150 GB download cost); Phase U redundant with Wave B's rollup-sync extension. Withdrawn-filter columns provisioned in v24 then dropped in v25 after verification showed vdb's OSV + GHSA sources skip withdrawn at ingest (appthreat-vulnerability-db/lib/osv.py:91 + gha.py:184-185). 5 parent-spec errors caught pre-implementation. Live: 213 EPSS-scored + 216 CWE-classified actionable feedstocks; CWE distribution Info-Disclosure 133 / DoS 24 / RCE 21 / Other 18 / Injection 9 / Traversal 5 / Auth-Bypass 4 / Memory-Safety 2.

cancelled_phases_history.T (blint hardening — local + admin top-N: CANCELLED 2026-05-23 pre-implementation (v8.6.0 Wave C verify-don't-assume): ~0 hardening variance in conda-forge's hermetic compile env; ~150 GB download cost in admin top-N mode; blint can't scan .conda archives directly; PyPI package name was wrong in parent spec (owasp-blint 404 vs blint).
cancelled_phases_history.U (EPSS overlay — pure-SQL tail step ove: CANCELLED 2026-05-23 pre-implementation (v8.6.0 Wave C verify-don't-assume): redundant with Wave B's _phase_g_sync_current_rollup extension which already propagates vuln_max_epss_score with COALESCE-to-existing. A genuine standalone Phase U requires a per-package CVE list / new package_cves table (filed as DW19, separate spec).

data_artifacts.cf_atlas.db: primary artifact (+ -shm, -wal); schema v28 includes packages + pypi_universe + pypi_intelligence + cisa_kev + epss_scores + cwe_categories + pypi_downloads_daily (v26) + package_platform_downloads/package_python_downloads (v27) + package_channel_downloads (v28) side tables; ~21 tables total
data_artifacts.cwe_categories_seed.json: committed at .claude/skills/conda-forge-expert/data/cwe_categories_seed.json — 67-entry CWE→cf_atlas 7-category mapping (RCE / DoS / Info-Disclosure / Auth-Bypass / Memory-Safety / Traversal / Injection); unmapped CWEs default to 'Other'. Read by cwe_catalog_fetcher.py at upsert time.
data_artifacts.cf-graph-countyfair.tar.gz: cf-graph offline snapshot (used by Phase E/H/M)
data_artifacts.vdb/, vdb-cache/: AppThreat vulnerability database (OSV + GHSA via vdb --cache; deliberately NOT --cache-os, see CHANGELOG v8.5.3 entry for the 33 GB-crash rationale)
data_artifacts.cache/parquet/: Phase F S3 monthly parquet cache (created on demand, ~13MB/month)

parts.description: FastMCP server exposing 42 MCP tools (verified by grep `@mcp.tool` 2026-06-20) across recipe-authoring, atlas-intelligence, and project-scanning surfaces. Since the prior sync added: pypi_intelligence (v8.1.0), platform_breakdown / pyver_breakdown / channel_split (v8.19.0 Wave-3 CLIs), download_pr_artifacts (v8.14.0 Azure DevOps), env_inspect. Auto-started by Claude Code at session boot. Each tool is a thin wrapper around a Tier-1 canonical script from Part 1.

tool_surfaces.recipe-authoring: ~15 tools: generate_recipe_from_pypi, validate_recipe, edit_recipe, optimize_recipe, scan_for_vulnerabilities, trigger_build, get_build_summary, analyze_build_failure, prepare_submission_branch, submit_pr, migrate_to_v1, update_recipe_from_github, update_recipe, update_cve_database, update_mapping_cache
tool_surfaces.atlas-intelligence: ~18 tools: query_atlas, package_health, staleness_report, cve_watcher, behind_upstream, feedstock_health, whodepends, release_cadence, version_downloads, find_alternative, adoption_stage, pypi_only_candidates, pypi_intelligence, platform_breakdown, pyver_breakdown, channel_split, my_feedstocks, lookup_feedstock, get_feedstock_context, enrich_from_feedstock, check_dependencies, check_github_version, get_conda_name
tool_surfaces.project-scanning: ~6 tools: scan_project (~28 input formats), run_system_health_check, env_inspect, download_pr_artifacts, get_build_summary, analyze_build_failure

parts.description: BMAD-METHOD v6.6.0 installer + multi-project planning layout. Six-layer config merge, 65 installed skills, active-project resolution via bmad-switch. Hosts multiple BMAD projects (local-recipes + sibling projects) in a single repo.
parts.installed_skills_note: Current repo state is 65 entries in .claude/skills/ but one (.claude/skills/data/) is a stray directory with no SKILL.md inside (contains only an old conda-forge-expert/ subdir). Real skill count is 64: 42 BMAD-installer + 21 engineering-practice + 1 repo-specific (conda-forge-expert). Rebuild target = 64 real skills; the stray is not recreated.

integration_points.details: Both parts read/write .claude/data/conda-forge-expert/ — cf_atlas.db is Part 2's primary artifact; Part 1's edit_recipe / scan_for_vulnerabilities / generate_recipe_from_pypi consume it via query helpers.

Each MCP tool in conda_forge_server.py imports from .claude/skills/conda-forge-expert/scripts/<module>.py and forwards args. The MCP server is the wire format; the canonical implementation is Part 1's scripts.
Atlas-intelligence tools (query_atlas, package_health, staleness_report, etc.) read cf_atlas.db directly via the canonical scripts in Part 1.

Every BMAD agent (planning, dev, review, retro) touching conda-forge work MUST invoke the conda-forge-expert skill via Skill tool. Codified in CLAUDE.md § 'BMAD ↔ conda-forge-expert integration'. The skill's 9-step loop + critical constraints override BMAD story instructions when they conflict.

Every BMAD effort that did conda-forge work runs bmad-retrospective at closeout. Retro findings update SKILL.md, reference/*.md, guides/*.md, and CHANGELOG.md. Skill version bumps per semver (PATCH for fixes, MINOR for new gotchas, MAJOR for breaking workflow changes). Codified in CLAUDE.md § 'BMAD ↔ conda-forge-expert integration'.

Every outbound HTTP request from Parts 1, 2, 3 routes through .claude/skills/conda-forge-expert/scripts/_http.py — truststore (native CA) + JFrog X-JFrog-Art-Api header (when JFROG_API_KEY set) + GitHub token + .netrc fallback. CRITICAL: JFROG_API_KEY leaks to every host when set; mitigation patterns in docs/enterprise-deployment.md § 2 Cross-host credential leak.

vuln-db pixi env provides AppThreat vulnerability database (NVD + GHSA + OSV + npm + Snyk). Part 1's scan_for_vulnerabilities and Part 2's Phase G/G' read from .claude/data/conda-forge-expert/vdb/. Separate env keeps the default local-recipes env lean (AppThreat pulls ~500MB).

rebuild_dependencies.reasoning: BMAD infra must exist for any planning to happen. The skill (Part 1) is foundational — Parts 2 and 3 are essentially extensions of it (Part 2 = the data pipeline encoded in the skill's scripts/; Part 3 = the MCP wire format over the skill's scripts/). Build order matches dependency order.
